hypothesis: Stronger L2 (C=0.5) on the linear model probes whether regularization closes the gap before deeper architectures are tried.
Gradient-boosted trees capture the non-linear lab interactions (Bilirubin/Albumin/Prothrombin) the linear model cannot; UCI original rows augment the rare CL class.
CatBoost handles the 6 clinical categoricals natively with ordered target statistics, avoiding one-hot information loss; expected strongest single model.
Distance-weighted KNN tests whether local similarity in standardized lab space carries signal; expected to be dominated but required for class coverage.
An RBF-kernel SVM probes a non-linear decision boundary distinct from trees; required for architecture coverage.
